content_markdown:
In the circuit below, the voltmeter has a resistance $100\text{ k}\Omega$. The battery has negligible internal resistance and e.m.f. $6\text{ V}$.

The reading on the voltmeter is

diagram_metadata.description: A circuit diagram showing a 6V battery connected to two 100kΩ resistors in series. A voltmeter with 100kΩ resistance is connected in parallel with the second resistor.

vibe_explanation: The voltmeter is in parallel with the bottom $100\text{ k}\Omega$ resistor. Two $100\text{ k}\Omega$ resistors in parallel have an equivalent resistance of $50\text{ k}\Omega$. Now we have a potential divider with $100\text{ k}\Omega$ (top) and $50\text{ k}\Omega$ (bottom). The voltage across the bottom part is $V = 6 \times \frac{50}{100 + 50} = 6 \times \frac{1}{3} = 2\text{ V}$.
